executiveSummary: The password reset flow is vulnerable to practical brute-force attacks due to missing rate limiting, lockout controls, and cross-session validation protections. The attack chain is fully unauthenticated and can lead to account takeover.
purpose: Validate whether the password reset mechanism can be abused to take over accounts and enumerate user information.
detailedAnalysis: Observed chain: ForgotPassword leaks PII and static TUG token -> SendCode can be triggered repeatedly -> ValidateCode accepts parallel, repeated wrong attempts with no lockout and no throttling -> cross-session validation succeeds. Measured throughput: ~11.25 req/sec using 5 concurrent connections.

findings.title: Password Reset Code Brute-Force Feasible via Parallel Cross-Session Requests with No Rate Limiting
findings.description: The password reset flow (ForgotPassword -> SendCode -> ValidateCode -> ResetPassword) on castatus.cloud lacks rate limiting, account lockout, and effective expiry after failed validation attempts. An unauthenticated attacker can retrieve a static TUG token, trigger code delivery repeatedly, brute-force ValidateCode in parallel sessions, and then reset victim passwords.
findings.poc:
$ node validate_finding.js
ForgotPassword leaks email/phone + static TUG (same across calls)
SendCode returns Success with no controls
ValidateCode: 50+ failed attempts all return HTTP 200 'Error'
Cross-session validation returns 200/Error (no session binding)
Parallel throughput: ~11.25 req/sec

$ node validate_benign.js
No rate-limit headers present
Unauthenticated ForgotPassword reveals PII
findings.impact: Unauthenticated account takeover is feasible for known-email users via reset-code brute force. User enumeration and PII disclosure (email + phone) are also possible. At ~11 req/sec, a 4-digit code can be brute-forced in ~15 minutes.
findings.remediation: Implement strict rate limiting and lockout on ValidateCode, expire reset codes within 10-15 minutes, bind validation to the originating session, generate per-request reset tokens, return generic ForgotPassword responses without PII, add CAPTCHA/challenge after failures, and alert on repeated failed validations.
